metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_756
metadata.comment: Rationale: Authors used a polyclonal antibody against AIDA-1 generated in rabbit to study the protein distribution in vivo. This antibody identifies at least different isoforms of AIDA-1 (AIDA-1d, AIDA-1e and EB-1), being AIDA-1d and 1e found in synaptosomes and PSD fractions. Confocal imaging revealed a prominent staining of AIDA1 in the stratum lucidum of CA3 which colocalized with VGLUT1 staining, suggesting the localization of AIDA1 in mossy fiber terminals. This was confirmed by subsequent preembedding immunoperoxidase experiments that showed immunolabeling of AIDA1 in mossy fiber terminals, concentrated at the center of synaptic vesicle pools.. Experimental description: Experiments were carried out on 13 adult male Sprague-Dawley rats ranging from 3 to 10 months old, from Charles River Laboratories (Raleigh, NC). Four were used for diaminobenzidine (DAB) staining, four were used for confocal microscopy, two were used for preembed- ding electron microscopy, and three were used for post- embedding electron microscopy.
